10	# TASK-0041: Show hex, HSL, and OKLCH values on each swatch
11	
12	Display all three color format values directly on each swatch: hex, HSL, and OKLCH. Currently swatches only show the HSL value.
13	
14	## Requirements
15	
16	- Each swatch should render hex, HSL, and OKLCH values as visible text (not just on hover/tooltip)
17	- Values should be legible against both light and dark swatch backgrounds (auto contrast)
18	- Typography should stay dense and compact per the design principles — small mono type, no extra chrome
19	- Copy-on-click behavior should still work — which format gets copied is determined by a format selector control above the swatch grid (hex, HSL, or OKLCH)
20	
21	## Format selector
22	
23	- Add a control above the swatch grid to choose the active copy format: hex, HSL, or OKLCH
24	- Clicking a swatch copies the value in the selected format
25	- Persist the selection in the Zustand store so it survives re-renders
26	
27	## Notes
28	
29	- This is a display-only change — it does not depend on the OKLCH migration (EPIC-0007). The current palette already has hex available, and OKLCH can be derived from the existing RGB/hex values via chroma.js.
30	- Related but distinct from TASK-0029, which focuses on updating the copy target and tooltip post-migration. This task is about showing all three formats simultaneously on the swatch face.
31
```

1	import { useMemo, useState } from "react";
2	import { ColorSwatch } from "./ColorSwatch";
3	import { ToggleSwitch } from "../controls/ToggleSwitch";
4	import { useCopyToClipboard } from "../../hooks/useCopyToClipboard";
5	import type { ColorEntry } from "../../types/palette";
6	
7	interface PaletteGridProps {
8	  entries: ColorEntry[];
9	}
10	
11	const STEP_OPTIONS = [8, 9, 10, 12, 16, 20, 24, 48];
12	
13	type Layout = "row" | "column" | "vertical" | "horizontal";
14	
15	function sampleEntries(entries: ColorEntry[], count: number): ColorEntry[] {
16	  if (count >= entries.length) return entries;
17	  const step = (entries.length - 1) / (count - 1);
18	  return Array.from({ length: count }, (_, i) =>
19	    entries[Math.round(i * step)],
20	  );
21	}
22	
23	// Find the column count that produces no orphans and the most square-ish grid
24	function bestCols(total: number): number {
25	  const target = Math.round(Math.sqrt(total));
26	  // Search outward from the square root for a clean divisor
27	  for (let d = 0; d <= target; d++) {
28	    if ((target + d) > 0 && total % (target + d) === 0) return target + d;
29	    if ((target - d) > 0 && total % (target - d) === 0) return target - d;
30	  }
31	  return target;
32	}
33	
34	// Reorder row-major entries into column-major order for CSS grid rendering
35	function toColumnMajor(entries: ColorEntry[], cols: number): ColorEntry[] {
36	  const rows = Math.ceil(entries.length / cols);
37	  const reordered: ColorEntry[] = [];
38	  for (let r = 0; r < rows; r++) {
39	    for (let c = 0; c < cols; c++) {
40	      const idx = c * rows + r;
41	      if (idx < entries.length) reordered.push(entries[idx]);
42	    }
43	  }
44	  return reordered;
45	}
46	
47	export function PaletteGrid({ entries }: PaletteGridProps) {
48	  const { copiedLabel, copy } = useCopyToClipboard();
49	  const [showBorders, setShowBorders] = useState(false);
50	  const [showLabels, setShowLabels] = useState(true);
51	  const [reversed, setReversed] = useState(false);
52	  const [swatchCount, setSwatchCount] = useState<number>(STEP_OPTIONS[0]);
53	  const [layout, setLayout] = useState<Layout>("row");
54	
55	  const { displayEntries, cols } = useMemo(() => {
56	    const sampled = sampleEntries(entries, swatchCount);
57	
58	    // Default order for all layouts is dark→light (reverse the light→dark entries)
59	    const ordered = reversed ? sampled : sampled.slice().reverse();
60	
61	    if (layout === "row") {
62	      return { displayEntries: ordered, cols: ordered.length };
63	    }
64	
65	    if (layout === "column") {
66	      return { displayEntries: ordered, cols: 1 };
67	    }
68	
69	    const cols = bestCols(ordered.length);
70	
71	    if (layout === "vertical") {
72	      return { displayEntries: ordered, cols };
73	    }
74	    // horizontal: lightness flows left→right via column-major reorder
75	    return { displayEntries: toColumnMajor(ordered, cols), cols };
76	  }, [entries, swatchCount, layout, reversed]);
77	
78	  return (
79	    <div className="flex flex-col">
80	      <div className="flex flex-wrap justify-end items-center gap-1 px-4 lg:pr-0 mb-3">
81	        <div className="flex items-center gap-3 rounded-md bg-surface-raised px-3 py-1.5">
82	          <label className="flex items-center gap-1 cursor-pointer">
83	            <span className="text-sm font-mono text-text-tertiary">swatches</span>
84	            <select
85	              value={swatchCount}
86	              onChange={(e) => setSwatchCount(Number(e.target.value))}
87	              className="text-sm font-mono text-text-subtle bg-transparent border-none outline-none cursor-pointer"
88	            >
89	              {STEP_OPTIONS.map((n) => (
90	                <option key={n} value={n} className="bg-surface-overlay">
91	                  {n}
92	                </option>
93	              ))}
94	              <option value={entries.length} className="bg-surface-overlay">
95	                all ({entries.length})
96	              </option>
97	            </select>
98	          </label>
99	          <label className="flex items-center gap-1 cursor-pointer">
100	            <span className="text-sm font-mono text-text-tertiary">layout</span>
101	            <select
102	              value={layout}
103	              onChange={(e) => setLayout(e.target.value as Layout)}
104	              className="text-sm font-mono text-text-subtle bg-transparent border-none outline-none cursor-pointer"
105	            >
106	              <option value="row" className="bg-surface-overlay">row</option>
107	              <option value="column" className="bg-surface-overlay">column</option>
108	              <option value="vertical" className="bg-surface-overlay">vertical</option>
109	              <option value="horizontal" className="bg-surface-overlay">horizontal</option>
110	            </select>
111	          </label>
112	        </div>
113	        <div className="flex items-center gap-3 rounded-md bg-surface-raised px-3 py-1.5">
114	          <ToggleSwitch label="reverse" checked={reversed} onChange={setReversed} />
115	          <ToggleSwitch label="labels" checked={showLabels} onChange={setShowLabels} />
116	          <ToggleSwitch label="borders" checked={showBorders} onChange={setShowBorders} />
117	        </div>
118	      </div>
119	      <div
120	        className={`grid flex-1 ml-4 mr-4 lg:mr-0 rounded-xl overflow-hidden auto-rows-fr ${showBorders ? "gap-px bg-surface-overlay" : "gap-0"}`}
121	        style={{ gridTemplateColumns: `repeat(${cols}, minmax(0, 1fr))` }}
122	      >
123	        {displayEntries.map((entry) => (
124	          <ColorSwatch
125	            key={entry.label}
126	            entry={entry}
127	            isCopied={copiedLabel === entry.label}
128	            onCopy={copy}
129	            showLabels={showLabels}
130	            tall={layout === "row"}
131	          />
132	        ))}
133	      </div>
134	    </div>
135	  );

1	import { useState, useCallback, useRef } from "react";
2	
3	export function useCopyToClipboard(timeout = 1500) {
4	  const [copiedLabel, setCopiedLabel] = useState<string | null>(null);
5	  const [error, setError] = useState<string | null>(null);
6	  const timerRef = useRef<ReturnType<typeof setTimeout>>(undefined);
7	
8	  const copy = useCallback(
9	    (text: string, label: string) => {
10	      navigator.clipboard
11	        .writeText(text)
12	        .then(() => {
13	          setCopiedLabel(label);
14	          setError(null);
15	          clearTimeout(timerRef.current);
16	          timerRef.current = setTimeout(() => setCopiedLabel(null), timeout);
17	        })
18	        .catch((err: unknown) => {
19	          setCopiedLabel(null);
20	          setError(err instanceof Error ? err.message : "Copy failed");
21	          console.warn("Clipboard write failed:", err);
22	        });
23	    },
24	    [timeout],
25	  );
26	
27	  return { copiedLabel, error, copy };
28	}
29
```
</details></tool-use>

```text
1	import chroma from "chroma-js";
2	import type { ColorEntry, PaletteParams, SatMode } from "../types/palette";
3	
4	/**
5	 * Parabolic saturation curve centered at lightness 50%.
6	 * See: https://www.desmos.com/calculator/02ufrfsuzy
7	 *
8	 * In "endpoint" mode the multiplier is 1.0 at L0/L100 (slider = endpoint saturation).
9	 * In "target" mode the multiplier is 1.0 at L50 (slider = vertex/target saturation).
10	 */
11	export function getSaturation(lightness: number, sMod: number, satMode: SatMode): number {
12	  if (sMod === 0) return 1;
13	  const o = 50;
14	  const raw = 1 + (Math.pow(lightness - o, 2) / sMod - Math.pow(o, 2) / sMod) / 100;
15	
16	  if (satMode === "endpoint") return raw;
17	
18	  // Normalize so vertex (L50) = 1.0, endpoints > 1.0
19	  const vertex = 1 - (Math.pow(o, 2) / sMod) / 100;
20	  if (vertex <= 0) return raw;
21	  return raw / vertex;
22	}
23	
24	export function generateNeutrals(params: PaletteParams): ColorEntry[] {
25	  const { hue, saturation, sMod, satMode, lMin, lMax } = params;
26	
27	  const entries: ColorEntry[] = [];
28	
29	  for (let i = lMin; i <= lMax; i++) {
30	    const l = i / 100;
31	    const sK = getSaturation(i, sMod, satMode);
32	    const color = chroma.hsl(hue, saturation * sK, l);
33	
34	    const [h, s, lVal] = color.hsl();
35	    const hRound = Math.round(h) || 0;
36	    const sRound = Math.round(s * 10000) / 100;
37	    const lRound = Math.round(lVal * 10000) / 100;
38	
39	    entries.push({
40	      label: `grey-${i}`,
41	      lightness: i,
42	      hsl: [h, s, lVal],
43	      hslString: `${hRound}deg ${sRound}% ${lRound}%`,
44	      hex: color.hex(),
45	    });
46	  }
47	
48	  return entries.reverse();
49	}
50
```
</details></tool-use>

1	import chroma from "chroma-js";
2	import type { ColorEntry, ExportFormat, ColorSpace } from "../types/palette";
3	
4	function colorValue(entry: ColorEntry, space: ColorSpace): string {
5	  const c = chroma(entry.hex);
6	  switch (space) {
7	    case "hsl":
8	      return `hsl(${entry.hslString})`;
9	    case "hex":
10	      return entry.hex;
11	    case "rgb": {
12	      const [r, g, b] = c.rgb();
13	      return `rgb(${r}, ${g}, ${b})`;
14	    }
15	    case "hcl": {

26	      const hR = Math.round(h || 0);
27	      return `oklch(${lR} ${cR} ${hR})`;
28	    }
29	  }

<tool-use data-tool-type="write" data-tool-name="Write"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **Write**</summary>

```tsx
import { getContrastColor, toOklchString } from "../../lib/colors";
import { CopyFeedback } from "./CopyFeedback";
import type { ColorEntry, CopyFormat } from "../../types/palette";

interface ColorSwatchProps {
  entry: ColorEntry;
  copyFormat: CopyFormat;
  isCopied: boolean;
  onCopy: (text: string, label: string) => void;
  showLabels?: boolean;
  tall?: boolean;
}

function getCopyValue(entry: ColorEntry, format: CopyFormat): string {
  switch (format) {
    case "hex":
      return entry.hex;
    case "hsl":
      return `hsl(${entry.hslString})`;
    case "oklch":
      return toOklchString(entry.hex);
  }
}

export function ColorSwatch({ entry, copyFormat, isCopied, onCopy, showLabels = true, tall = false }: ColorSwatchProps) {
  const textColor = getContrastColor(entry.hex);
  const oklch = toOklchString(entry.hex);

  return (
    <button
      type="button"
      className={`relative flex flex-col justify-start gap-0.5 text-left cursor-pointer focus-visible:outline-none focus-visible:ring-2 focus-visible:ring-white/50 focus-visible:ring-offset-1 focus-visible:ring-offset-surface-base group px-2 py-1.5 ${tall ? "min-h-[240px]" : "min-h-[120px]"}`}
      style={{ backgroundColor: entry.hex, color: textColor }}
      onClick={() => onCopy(getCopyValue(entry, copyFormat), entry.label)}
      title={`${entry.label} — click to copy ${copyFormat.toUpperCase()}`}
    >
      {showLabels && (
        <>
          <span className="text-sm font-semibold transition-transform group-hover:scale-105 origin-left">{entry.label}</span>
          <span className="text-2xs font-mono opacity-75">{entry.hex}</span>
          <span className="text-2xs font-mono opacity-75">hsl({entry.hslString})</span>
          <span className="text-2xs font-mono opacity-75">{oklch}</span>
        </>
      )}
      <CopyFeedback visible={isCopied} />
    </button>
  );
}

```

+export function toOklchString(hex: string): string {
+  const [l, c, h] = chroma(hex).oklch();
+  const lR = Math.round(l * 1000) / 1000;

## Steps

### 1. Review Board State

Parse the pre-loaded board data above. Identify:
- All items with `status: backlog` or `status: current` (candidates for completion)
- Epics and their child tasks (via `parent` field)
- Blocked items and their blockers
- Items with terminal status (`done`, `processed`) that should be archived

### 2. Gather Codebase Context

- Run `git log --oneline` for recent commits (last 30) to understand what work has been done
- Only Read full file bodies for tasks whose status is `backlog` or `current` AND whose title suggests overlap with recent commits
- Use the context budget escalation (Glob → Grep → Read with limit → Full read) for any additional lookups

### 3. Detect Completed Work

For each task with `status: backlog` or `status: current`, check if the work described has already been done:

- Look at recent commits for evidence the work was completed
- Check the codebase for the feature/fix the task describes
- If the work is done, flag it for status update to `done`

### 4. Detect New Work Needed

Scan for things that should become tasks but aren't tracked yet:

- Check recent commits for TODOs or FIXMEs introduced in code
- Look at open epics — are there obvious next steps not yet captured as tasks?
- Check if any `blocked_by` dependencies have been resolved (unblocking tasks)

### 5. Update Epic Progress

For each active epic:

- Check if all child tasks are `done` — if so, flag the epic as `done`
- Check if any child tasks are `current` — if so, the epic should be `current`
- Update the task list in the epic body if tasks have been added/removed

### 6. Check for Stale Items

- Tasks marked `current` but with no related commits in the last 30 days
- Epics in `planning` with no child tasks
- Resources stuck in `inbox` or `processing`

### 7. Present Changes

Present ALL proposed changes as a single summary before making any edits:

```
## Board Update Proposal

### Status Changes
- TASK-0003: backlog -> done (cache-decoded-image: implemented in commit a1b2c3d)
- EPIC-0001: planning -> current (has active child tasks)

### New Tasks Suggested
- "Fix FIXME in preprocessing.rs line 42" (found in recent commit)

### Ready to Archive
- TASK-0003: done
- NOTE-0002: processed

### Stale Items
- TASK-0007: marked current but no activity in 45 days

### Unblocked
- TASK-0005: blocker TASK-0003 is now done
```

**Avoid redundant invocations** — if this skill was already invoked earlier in the session and the board has not been modified since, reuse the previous board data instead of invoking again.

Ask the user to approve, reject, or cherry-pick which changes to apply. Then execute only the approved changes:

- **Status updates**: Use `/board:board-edit` to update status fields
- **New tasks**: Invoke `/board:board-add` for each approved new task
- **Archiving**: Run `bash /Users/grant/Documents/Projects/BoardPM/plugin/scripts/archive-batch.sh ./board <file1> <file2> ...` directly for approved items

_**User (2026-04-01 10:15:40Z)**_

what was that about 5 swatches being weird?

---

_**Agent (claude-opus-4-6 2026-04-01 10:15:47Z)**_



In the "vertical" and "horizontal" grid layouts, `bestCols` picks the most square-ish divisor. Since 5 is prime, the only clean divisor is 1 — so it renders as a single column of 5 rows instead of a 2-3 type grid.

It doesn't affect the default "row" layout (all swatches in one row) or "column" layout (always 1 column). It only looks slightly odd if someone picks 5 swatches + vertical/horizontal grid — they'd get a tall strip instead of a grid.

Probably fine since there's no clean 2D grid for 5 anyway. Want me to leave it as-is or handle the prime case differently?

---

None of the existing options are prime except 3 and 5, and at those sizes a single row is the natural result anyway. The `bestCols` fallback to `target` (the square root) handles it — 5 just happens to fall back to 1 because `round(sqrt(5))` is 2, and neither 2±d divides 5 cleanly until d reaches 1 (giving cols=1).

_**Agent (claude-opus-4-6 2026-04-01 10:28:59Z)**_

The two issues:
1. **Left-side click dead zone**: The label `<span>` next to the `<select>` receives the click, but clicking a `<label>` only opens a `<select>` if the select has an `id` and the label has a matching `htmlFor`. With `flex items-center gap-1`, the span is adjacent but not wired.
2. **Touch target too small**: The selects and toggles need `min-h-[24px]` (CLAUDE.md says 24px web minimum).

_**User (2026-04-01 10:35:56Z)**_

ok does it make sense to add some JS/React on the label that opens the select?

---

_**Agent (claude-opus-4-6 2026-04-01 10:36:03Z)**_



Not really — you can't programmatically open a native `<select>` dropdown. There's no DOM API for it (`select.showPicker()` only exists for `<input type="date">` and similar). You'd have to either:

1. Replace the native `<select>` with a custom dropdown (Radix Select), which is a bigger change
2. Use `mousedown` + `select.focus()` on the label, which still only focuses without opening

The label-next-to-select pattern is standard and expected. Users know to click the select itself. The label is there for a11y and visual context, not as a click target for the dropdown.

---

The tradeoff is more markup and you lose the native `<select>` feel (scroll behavior, mobile OS sheet), but for this use case with small option lists it's a clean fit.

1	import { useMemo, useState } from "react";
2	import { ColorSwatch } from "./ColorSwatch";
3	import { ToggleSwitch } from "../controls/ToggleSwitch";
4	import { useCopyToClipboard } from "../../hooks/useCopyToClipboard";
5	import { usePaletteStore } from "../../store/paletteStore";
6	import type { ColorEntry, CopyFormat } from "../../types/palette";
7	
8	interface PaletteGridProps {
9	  entries: ColorEntry[];
10	}
11	
12	const STEP_OPTIONS = [3, 4, 5, 8, 9, 10, 12, 16, 20, 24, 48];
13	
14	type Layout = "row" | "column" | "vertical" | "horizontal";
15	
16	function sampleEntries(entries: ColorEntry[], count: number): ColorEntry[] {
17	  if (count >= entries.length) return entries;
18	  const step = (entries.length - 1) / (count - 1);
19	  return Array.from({ length: count }, (_, i) =>
20	    entries[Math.round(i * step)],
21	  );
22	}
23	
24	// Find the column count that produces no orphans and the most square-ish grid
25	function bestCols(total: number): number {
26	  if (total <= 1) return 1;
27	  const target = Math.round(Math.sqrt(total));
28	  // Search outward from the square root for a clean divisor (min 2 cols)
29	  for (let d = 0; d <= target; d++) {
30	    if ((target + d) >= 2 && total % (target + d) === 0) return target + d;
31	    if ((target - d) >= 2 && total % (target - d) === 0) return target - d;
32	  }
33	  return total;
34	}
35	
36	// Reorder row-major entries into column-major order for CSS grid rendering
37	function toColumnMajor(entries: ColorEntry[], cols: number): ColorEntry[] {
38	  const rows = Math.ceil(entries.length / cols);
39	  const reordered: ColorEntry[] = [];
40	  for (let r = 0; r < rows; r++) {
41	    for (let c = 0; c < cols; c++) {
42	      const idx = c * rows + r;
43	      if (idx < entries.length) reordered.push(entries[idx]);
44	    }
45	  }
46	  return reordered;
47	}
48	
49	export function PaletteGrid({ entries }: PaletteGridProps) {
50	  const { copiedLabel, copy } = useCopyToClipboard();
51	  const copyFormat = usePaletteStore((s) => s.copyFormat);
52	  const setCopyFormat = usePaletteStore((s) => s.setCopyFormat);
53	  const [showBorders, setShowBorders] = useState(false);
54	  const [showLabels, setShowLabels] = useState(true);
55	  const [reversed, setReversed] = useState(false);
56	  const [swatchCount, setSwatchCount] = useState<number>(STEP_OPTIONS[0]);
57	  const [layout, setLayout] = useState<Layout>("row");
58	
59	  const { displayEntries, cols } = useMemo(() => {
60	    const sampled = sampleEntries(entries, swatchCount);
61	
62	    // Default order for all layouts is dark→light (reverse the light→dark entries)
63	    const ordered = reversed ? sampled : sampled.slice().reverse();
64	
65	    if (layout === "row") {
66	      return { displayEntries: ordered, cols: ordered.length };
67	    }
68	
69	    if (layout === "column") {
70	      return { displayEntries: ordered, cols: 1 };
71	    }
72	
73	    const cols = bestCols(ordered.length);
74	
75	    if (layout === "vertical") {
76	      return { displayEntries: ordered, cols };
77	    }
78	    // horizontal: lightness flows left→right via column-major reorder
79	    return { displayEntries: toColumnMajor(ordered, cols), cols };
80	  }, [entries, swatchCount, layout, reversed]);
81	
82	  return (
83	    <div className="flex flex-col">
84	      <div className="flex flex-wrap justify-end items-center gap-1 px-4 lg:pr-0 mb-3">
85	        <div className="flex items-center gap-3 rounded-md bg-surface-raised px-3 py-1.5">
86	          <div className="flex items-center gap-1">
87	            <label htmlFor="grid-swatches" className="text-sm font-mono text-text-tertiary cursor-pointer">swatches</label>
88	            <select
89	              id="grid-swatches"
90	              value={swatchCount}
91	              onChange={(e) => setSwatchCount(Number(e.target.value))}
92	              className="text-sm font-mono text-text-subtle bg-transparent border-none outline-none cursor-pointer min-h-6 text-right"
93	            >
94	              {STEP_OPTIONS.map((n) => (
95	                <option key={n} value={n} className="bg-surface-overlay">
96	                  {n}
97	                </option>
98	              ))}
99	              <option value={entries.length} className="bg-surface-overlay">
100	                all ({entries.length})
101	              </option>
102	            </select>
103	          </div>
104	          <div className="flex items-center gap-1">
105	            <label htmlFor="grid-layout" className="text-sm font-mono text-text-tertiary cursor-pointer">layout</label>
106	            <select
107	              id="grid-layout"
108	              value={layout}
109	              onChange={(e) => setLayout(e.target.value as Layout)}
110	              className="text-sm font-mono text-text-subtle bg-transparent border-none outline-none cursor-pointer min-h-6 text-right"
111	            >
112	              <option value="row" className="bg-surface-overlay">row</option>
113	              <option value="column" className="bg-surface-overlay">column</option>
114	              <option value="vertical" className="bg-surface-overlay">vertical</option>
115	              <option value="horizontal" className="bg-surface-overlay">horizontal</option>
116	            </select>
117	          </div>
118	        </div>
119	        <div className="flex items-center gap-3 rounded-md bg-surface-raised px-3 py-1.5">
120	          <div className="flex items-center gap-1">
121	            <label htmlFor="grid-copy-format" className="text-sm font-mono text-text-tertiary cursor-pointer">copy</label>
122	            <select
123	              id="grid-copy-format"

```text
### Install Radix UI Select Component

Source: https://www.radix-ui.com/primitives/docs/components/select

Installs the Radix UI Select component using npm. This is the first step to integrating the select functionality into your project.

```bash
npm install @radix-ui/react-select
```

--------------------------------

### Select ItemText API

Source: https://www.radix-ui.com/primitives/docs/components/select

The textual part of the item. It should only contain the text you want to see in the trigger when that item is selected.

```APIDOC
## Select ItemText API

### Description
The textual part of the item. It should only contain the text you want to see in the trigger when that item is selected. It should not be styled to ensure correct positioning.

### Method
N/A (Component API)

### Endpoint
N/A (Component API)

### Parameters
#### Props
- **asChild** (boolean) - Optional - Defaults to `false`.

### Request Example
N/A (Component API)

### Response
#### Success Response (200)
N/A (Component API)

#### Response Example
N/A (Component API)
```

--------------------------------

### Select Item API

Source: https://www.radix-ui.com/primitives/docs/components/select

The component that contains the select items. It defines the value, and can be disabled.

```APIDOC
## Select Item API

### Description
The component that contains the select items.

### Method
N/A (Component API)

### Endpoint
N/A (Component API)

### Parameters
#### Props
- **asChild** (boolean) - Optional - Defaults to `false`.
- **value*** (string) - Required - The value of the item.
- **disabled** (boolean) - Optional - No default value.
- **textValue** (string) - Optional - No default value.

#### Data Attributes
- **[data-state]** (`"checked" | "unchecked"`) - Indicates if the item is checked.
- **[data-highlighted]** - Present when the item is highlighted.
- **[data-disabled]** - Present when the item is disabled.

### Request Example
N/A (Component API)

### Response
#### Success Response (200)
N/A (Component API)

#### Response Example
N/A (Component API)
```

--------------------------------

### Select ItemIndicator API

Source: https://www.radix-ui.com/primitives/docs/components/select

Renders when the item is selected. You can style this element directly, or you can use it as a wrapper to put an icon into, or both.

```APIDOC
## Select ItemIndicator API

### Description
Renders when the item is selected. You can style this element directly, or you can use it as a wrapper to put an icon into, or both.

### Method
N/A (Component API)

### Endpoint
N/A (Component API)

### Parameters
#### Props
- **asChild** (boolean) - Optional - Defaults to `false`.

### Request Example
N/A (Component API)

### Response
#### Success Response (200)
N/A (Component API)

#### Response Example
N/A (Component API)
```

--------------------------------

### Radix UI Select with Controlled Value (JSX)

Source: https://www.radix-ui.com/primitives/docs/components/select

Illustrates how to control the selected value displayed in the Radix UI Select trigger using the `value` and `onValueChange` props. This allows for programmatic control over the select's state and custom display of the selected item.

```jsx
const countries = { france: "🇫🇷", "united-kingdom": "🇬🇧", spain: "🇪🇸" };

export default () => {
  const [value, setValue] = React.useState("france");

  return (
    <Select.Root value={value} onValueChange={setValue}>
      <Select.Trigger>
        <Select.Value aria-label={value}>{countries[value]}</Select.Value>

        <Select.Icon />
      </Select.Trigger>

      <Select.Portal>
        <Select.Content>
          <Select.Viewport>
            <Select.Item value="france">
              <Select.ItemText>France</Select.ItemText>

              <Select.ItemIndicator>…</Select.ItemIndicator>
            </Select.Item>

            <Select.Item value="united-kingdom">
              <Select.ItemText>United Kingdom</Select.ItemText>

              <Select.ItemIndicator>…</Select.ItemIndicator>
            </Select.Item>

            <Select.Item value="spain">
              <Select.ItemText>Spain</Select.ItemText>

              <Select.ItemIndicator>…</Select.ItemIndicator>
            </Select.Item>
          </Select.Viewport>
        </Select.Content>
      </Select.Portal>
    </Select.Root>
  );
};
```

--------------------------------

### Select Viewport API

Source: https://www.radix-ui.com/primitives/docs/components/select

The scrolling viewport that contains all of the items in the select component.

```APIDOC

### Description
The scrolling viewport that contains all of the items.

### Method

### Parameters
#### Props
- **asChild** (boolean) - Optional - Defaults to `false`.

### Request Example
N/A (Component API)

#### Success Response (200)

#### Response Example
N/A (Component API)
```

--------------------------------

### Add Placeholder to Select Value (React & CSS)

Source: https://www.radix-ui.com/primitives/docs/components/select

Demonstrates how to use the `placeholder` prop on Select.Value to display text when the select has no selected value. It also shows how to style the trigger using the `data-placeholder` attribute in CSS. Requires importing Select from 'radix-ui' and a CSS file.

```jsx
import { Select } from "radix-ui";

import "./styles.css";

export default () => (
  <Select.Root>
    <Select.Trigger className="SelectTrigger">
      <Select.Value placeholder="Pick an option" />

      <Select.Icon />
    </Select.Trigger>

    <Select.Portal>
      <Select.Content>…</Select.Content>
    </Select.Portal>
  </Select.Root>
);
```

```css
/* styles.css */

.SelectTrigger[data-placeholder] {
  color: "gainsboro";
}
```

--------------------------------

### Select Content API

Source: https://www.radix-ui.com/primitives/docs/components/select

The component that pops out when the select is open. It accepts various props to control its behavior and appearance.

```APIDOC
## Select Content API

### Description
The component that pops out when the select is open.

### Method
N/A (Component API)

### Endpoint
N/A (Component API)

### Parameters
#### Props
- **asChild** (boolean) - Optional - Defaults to `false`.
- **onCloseAutoFocus** (function) - Optional - No default value.
- **onEscapeKeyDown** (function) - Optional - No default value.
- **onPointerDownOutside** (function) - Optional - No default value.
- **position** (enum) - Optional - Defaults to `"item-aligned"`. Possible values: `"item-aligned"`, `"popper"`.
- **side** (enum) - Optional - Defaults to `"bottom"`. Possible values: `"top"`, `"right"`, `"bottom"`, `"left"`.
- **sideOffset** (number) - Optional - Defaults to `0`.
- **align** (enum) - Optional - Defaults to `"start"`. Possible values: `"start"`, `"center"`, `"end"`.
- **alignOffset** (number) - Optional - Defaults to `0`.
- **avoidCollisions** (boolean) - Optional - Defaults to `true`.
- **collisionBoundary** (Boundary) - Optional - Defaults to `[]`.
- **collisionPadding** (number | Padding) - Optional - Defaults to `10`.
- **arrowPadding** (number) - Optional - Defaults to `0`.
- **sticky** (enum) - Optional - Defaults to `"partial"`. Possible values: `"normal"`, `"always"`, `"partial"`.
- **hideWhenDetached** (boolean) - Optional - Defaults to `false`.

#### Data Attributes
- **[data-state]** (`"open" | "closed"`) - Indicates the open/closed state of the select.
- **[data-side]** (`"left" | "right" | "bottom" | "top"`) - Indicates the side the content is positioned on.
- **[data-align]** (`"start" | "end" | "center"`) - Indicates the alignment of the content.

#### CSS Variables
- **--radix-select-content-transform-origin**: The `transform-origin` computed from the content and arrow positions/offsets. Only present when `position="popper"`.
- **--radix-select-content-available-width**: The remaining width between the trigger and the boundary edge. Only present when `position="popper"`.
- **--radix-select-content-available-height**: The remaining height between the trigger and the boundary edge. Only present when `position="popper"`.
- **--radix-select-trigger-width**: The width of the trigger. Only present when `position="popper"`.
- **--radix-select-trigger-height**: The height of the trigger. Only present when `position="popper"`.

### Request Example
N/A (Component API)

### Response
#### Success Response (200)
N/A (Component API)

#### Response Example
N/A (Component API)
```

--------------------------------

### Change Select Positioning Mode (React)

Source: https://www.radix-ui.com/primitives/docs/components/select

Demonstrates how to change the positioning mode of the Select component to 'popper' for alternative alignment options, similar to Popover or DropdownMenu. This requires importing the Select component from 'radix-ui'.

```jsx
import { Select } from "radix-ui";

export default () => (
  <Select.Root>
    <Select.Trigger>…</Select.Trigger>

    <Select.Portal>
      <Select.Content position="popper" sideOffset={5}>
        …
      </Select.Content>
    </Select.Portal>
  </Select.Root>
);
```

--------------------------------

### Custom Select API Implementation and Usage

Source: https://www.radix-ui.com/primitives/docs/components/select

Provides an example of creating a custom Select API by abstracting Radix UI's primitive components. It includes both the implementation of the custom Select and SelectItem components and their usage in an application.

```jsx
import { Select, SelectItem } from "./your-select";

export default () => (
  <Select defaultValue="2">
    <SelectItem value="1">Item 1</SelectItem>

    <SelectItem value="2">Item 2</SelectItem>

    <SelectItem value="3">Item 3</SelectItem>
  </Select>
);
```

```jsx
// your-select.jsx

import * as React from "react";

import { Select as SelectPrimitive } from "radix-ui";

import { CheckIcon, ChevronDownIcon, ChevronUpIcon } from "@radix-ui/react-icons";

export const Select = React.forwardRef(({ children, ...props }, forwardedRef) => {
  return (
    <SelectPrimitive.Root {...props}>
      <SelectPrimitive.Trigger ref={forwardedRef}>
        <SelectPrimitive.Value />

        <SelectPrimitive.Icon>
          <ChevronDownIcon />
        </SelectPrimitive.Icon>
      </SelectPrimitive.Trigger>

      <SelectPrimitive.Portal>
        <SelectPrimitive.Content>
          <SelectPrimitive.ScrollUpButton>
            <ChevronUpIcon />
          </SelectPrimitive.ScrollUpButton>

          <SelectPrimitive.Viewport>{children}</SelectPrimitive.Viewport>

          <SelectPrimitive.ScrollDownButton>
            <ChevronDownIcon />
          </SelectPrimitive.ScrollDownButton>
        </SelectPrimitive.Content>
      </SelectPrimitive.Portal>
    </SelectPrimitive.Root>
  );
});

export const SelectItem = React.forwardRef(({ children, ...props }, forwardedRef) => {
  return (
    <SelectPrimitive.Item {...props} ref={forwardedRef}>
      <SelectPrimitive.ItemText>{children}</SelectPrimitive.ItemText>

      <SelectPrimitive.ItemIndicator>
        <CheckIcon />
      </SelectPrimitive.ItemIndicator>
    </SelectPrimitive.Item>
  );
});
```
```

```tsx
import * as Select from "@radix-ui/react-select";

interface InlineSelectProps<T extends string> {
  label: string;
  value: T;
  onChange: (value: T) => void;
  options: { value: T; label: string }[];
}

export function InlineSelect<T extends string>({ label, value, onChange, options }: InlineSelectProps<T>) {
  const selectedLabel = options.find((o) => o.value === value)?.label ?? value;

  return (
    <Select.Root value={value} onValueChange={onChange}>
      <Select.Trigger className="flex items-center gap-1 min-h-6 cursor-pointer text-sm font-mono outline-none">
        <span className="text-text-tertiary">{label}</span>
        <Select.Value>
          <span className="text-text-subtle">{selectedLabel}</span>
        </Select.Value>
      </Select.Trigger>
      <Select.Portal>
        <Select.Content
          position="popper"
          sideOffset={4}
          className="rounded-md bg-surface-overlay border border-border-default py-1 shadow-lg"
        >
          <Select.Viewport>
            {options.map((opt) => (
              <Select.Item
                key={opt.value}
                value={opt.value}
                className="flex items-center px-3 min-h-6 text-sm font-mono text-text-subtle cursor-pointer outline-none data-[highlighted]:bg-surface-hover"
              >
                <Select.ItemText>{opt.label}</Select.ItemText>
              </Select.Item>
            ))}
          </Select.Viewport>
        </Select.Content>
      </Select.Portal>
    </Select.Root>
  );
}

```

_**Agent (claude-opus-4-6 2026-04-01 10:46:20Z)**_

The cleanest CSS-only fix: render all option labels invisibly stacked so the container always sizes to the widest one.

_**Agent (claude-opus-4-6 2026-04-01 10:46:26Z)**_

<tool-use data-tool-type="write" data-tool-name="Write"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **Write**</summary>

```tsx
import * as Select from "@radix-ui/react-select";

interface InlineSelectProps<T extends string> {
  label: string;
  value: T;
  onChange: (value: T) => void;
  options: { value: T; label: string }[];
}

export function InlineSelect<T extends string>({ label, value, onChange, options }: InlineSelectProps<T>) {
  const selectedLabel = options.find((o) => o.value === value)?.label ?? value;

  return (
    <Select.Root value={value} onValueChange={onChange}>
      <Select.Trigger className="flex items-center gap-1 min-h-6 cursor-pointer text-sm font-mono outline-none">
        <span className="text-text-tertiary">{label}</span>
        <span className="relative inline-grid items-center">
          {/* Invisible sizer: stacks all labels so width matches the longest */}
          {options.map((opt) => (
            <span key={opt.value} className="invisible col-start-1 row-start-1 text-text-subtle" aria-hidden="true">
              {opt.label}
            </span>
          ))}
          <Select.Value className="col-start-1 row-start-1">
            <span className="text-text-subtle">{selectedLabel}</span>
          </Select.Value>
        </span>
      </Select.Trigger>
      <Select.Portal>
        <Select.Content
          position="popper"
          sideOffset={4}
          className="rounded-md bg-surface-overlay border border-border-default py-1 shadow-lg"
        >
          <Select.Viewport>
            {options.map((opt) => (
              <Select.Item
                key={opt.value}
                value={opt.value}
                className="flex items-center px-3 min-h-6 text-sm font-mono text-text-subtle cursor-pointer outline-none data-[highlighted]:bg-surface-hover"
              >
                <Select.ItemText>{opt.label}</Select.ItemText>
              </Select.Item>
            ))}
          </Select.Viewport>
        </Select.Content>
      </Select.Portal>
    </Select.Root>
  );
}

```

_**Agent (claude-opus-4-6 2026-04-01 10:49:34Z)**_



The label and value are still wrapping to two lines. The issue is the Radix `Select.Trigger` renders as a `button` which has default styles that may cause wrapping. Let me inspect what's actually happening and simplify.

_**Agent (claude-opus-4-6 2026-04-01 10:49:39Z)**_

<tool-use data-tool-type="write" data-tool-name="Write"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **Write**</summary>

```tsx
import * as Select from "@radix-ui/react-select";

interface InlineSelectProps<T extends string> {
  label: string;
  value: T;
  onChange: (value: T) => void;
  options: { value: T; label: string }[];
}

export function InlineSelect<T extends string>({ label, value, onChange, options }: InlineSelectProps<T>) {
  const selectedLabel = options.find((o) => o.value === value)?.label ?? value;
  const longestLabel = options.reduce((a, b) => (a.label.length > b.label.length ? a : b)).label;

  return (
    <Select.Root value={value} onValueChange={onChange}>
      <Select.Trigger asChild>
        <button type="button" className="inline-flex items-baseline gap-1 min-h-6 cursor-pointer text-sm font-mono whitespace-nowrap bg-transparent border-none outline-none">
          <span className="text-text-tertiary">{label}</span>
          <span className="relative text-text-subtle">
            {/* Invisible sizer for stable width */}
            <span className="invisible" aria-hidden="true">{longestLabel}</span>
            <span className="absolute inset-0">{selectedLabel}</span>
          </span>
        </button>
      </Select.Trigger>
      <Select.Portal>
        <Select.Content
          position="popper"
          sideOffset={4}
          className="rounded-md bg-surface-overlay border border-border-default py-1 shadow-lg"
        >
          <Select.Viewport>
            {options.map((opt) => (
              <Select.Item
                key={opt.value}
                value={opt.value}
                className="flex items-center px-3 min-h-6 text-sm font-mono text-text-subtle cursor-pointer outline-none data-[highlighted]:bg-surface-hover"
              >
                <Select.ItemText>{opt.label}</Select.ItemText>
              </Select.Item>
            ))}
          </Select.Viewport>
        </Select.Content>
      </Select.Portal>
    </Select.Root>
  );
}

```

 `./app/src/components/controls/InlineSelect.tsx`

```text
1	import * as Select from "@radix-ui/react-select";
2	
3	interface InlineSelectProps<T extends string> {
4	  label: string;
5	  value: T;
6	  onChange: (value: T) => void;
7	  options: { value: T; label: string }[];
8	}
9	
10	export function InlineSelect<T extends string>({ label, value, onChange, options }: InlineSelectProps<T>) {
11	  const selectedLabel = options.find((o) => o.value === value)?.label ?? value;
12	  const longestLabel = options.reduce((a, b) => (a.label.length > b.label.length ? a : b)).label;
13	
14	  return (
15	    <Select.Root value={value} onValueChange={onChange}>
16	      <Select.Trigger asChild>
17	        <button type="button" className="group inline-flex items-baseline gap-1 min-h-6 cursor-pointer text-sm font-mono whitespace-nowrap outline-none bg-surface-raised border border-border-default px-3 py-1.5 transition-colors">
18	          <span className="text-text-tertiary group-hover:text-white group-active:text-white transition-colors">{label}</span>
19	          <span className="relative text-text-subtle text-right group-hover:text-white group-active:text-white transition-colors">
20	            {/* Invisible sizer for stable width */}
21	            <span className="invisible" aria-hidden="true">{longestLabel}</span>
22	            <span className="absolute inset-0 text-right">{selectedLabel}</span>
23	          </span>
24	        </button>
25	      </Select.Trigger>
26	      <Select.Portal>
27	        <Select.Content
28	          position="popper"
29	          sideOffset={-1}
30	          align="end"
31	          className="bg-surface-raised border border-t-0 border-border-default shadow-lg"
32	        >
33	          <Select.Viewport>
34	            {options.map((opt) => (
35	              <Select.Item
36	                key={opt.value}
37	                value={opt.value}
38	                className="flex items-center justify-end px-3 min-h-6 text-sm font-mono text-text-subtle cursor-pointer outline-none data-[highlighted]:bg-surface-hover"
39	              >
40	                <Select.ItemText>{opt.label}</Select.ItemText>
41	              </Select.Item>
42	            ))}
43	          </Select.Viewport>
44	        </Select.Content>
45	      </Select.Portal>
46	    </Select.Root>
47	  );
48	}
49
```
</details></tool-use>

9	export function ToggleSwitch({ label, checked, onChange }: ToggleSwitchProps) {
10	  const id = useId();
11	  return (
12	    <label className="group flex items-center gap-1 cursor-pointer select-none bg-surface-raised border border-border-default px-3 py-1.5 transition-colors">

15	      </span>
16	      <button
17	        type="button"
18	        role="switch"
19	        aria-checked={checked}
20	        aria-labelledby={id}
21	        onClick={() => onChange(!checked)}
22	        className="relative w-7 h-4 rounded-full transition-colors min-w-[28px]"

34	        />
35	      </button>

37	  );

_**Agent (claude-opus-4-6 2026-04-01 11:47:42Z)**_

When unchecked, the thumb nudges 2px right on hover (toward "on"). When checked, it pulls back 2px left on hover (toward "off"). Both hint at what clicking will do.

---

_**User (2026-04-01 11:49:03Z)**_

is there any precedent for this?

---

_**Agent (claude-opus-4-6 2026-04-01 11:49:08Z)**_



Honestly, not really. It's a custom micro-interaction — I haven't seen it in any established component libraries or design systems. Standard toggle hover patterns are usually opacity changes, background shifts, or thumb scale.

The risk is it could feel fidgety or confusing — a thumb that moves without clicking might read as buggy rather than intentional. Want me to revert it?

## Steps

### 1. Review Board State

Parse the pre-loaded board data above. Identify:
- All items with `status: backlog` or `status: current` (candidates for completion)
- Epics and their child tasks (via `parent` field)
- Blocked items and their blockers
- Items with terminal status (`done`, `processed`) that should be archived

### 2. Gather Codebase Context

- Run `git log --oneline` for recent commits (last 30) to understand what work has been done
- Only Read full file bodies for tasks whose status is `backlog` or `current` AND whose title suggests overlap with recent commits
- Use the context budget escalation (Glob → Grep → Read with limit → Full read) for any additional lookups

### 3. Detect Completed Work

For each task with `status: backlog` or `status: current`, check if the work described has already been done:

- Look at recent commits for evidence the work was completed
- Check the codebase for the feature/fix the task describes
- If the work is done, flag it for status update to `done`

### 4. Detect New Work Needed

Scan for things that should become tasks but aren't tracked yet:

- Check recent commits for TODOs or FIXMEs introduced in code
- Look at open epics — are there obvious next steps not yet captured as tasks?
- Check if any `blocked_by` dependencies have been resolved (unblocking tasks)

### 5. Update Epic Progress

For each active epic:

- Check if all child tasks are `done` — if so, flag the epic as `done`
- Check if any child tasks are `current` — if so, the epic should be `current`
- Update the task list in the epic body if tasks have been added/removed

### 6. Check for Stale Items

- Tasks marked `current` but with no related commits in the last 30 days
- Epics in `planning` with no child tasks
- Resources stuck in `inbox` or `processing`

### 7. Present Changes

Present ALL proposed changes as a single summary before making any edits:

```
## Board Update Proposal

### Status Changes
- TASK-0003: backlog -> done (cache-decoded-image: implemented in commit a1b2c3d)
- EPIC-0001: planning -> current (has active child tasks)

### New Tasks Suggested
- "Fix FIXME in preprocessing.rs line 42" (found in recent commit)

### Ready to Archive
- TASK-0003: done
- NOTE-0002: processed

### Stale Items
- TASK-0007: marked current but no activity in 45 days

### Unblocked
- TASK-0005: blocker TASK-0003 is now done
```

**Avoid redundant invocations** — if this skill was already invoked earlier in the session and the board has not been modified since, reuse the previous board data instead of invoking again.

Ask the user to approve, reject, or cherry-pick which changes to apply. Then execute only the approved changes:

- **Status updates**: Use `/board:board-edit` to update status fields
- **New tasks**: Invoke `/board:board-add` for each approved new task
- **Archiving**: Run `bash /Users/grant/Documents/Projects/BoardPM/plugin/scripts/archive-batch.sh ./board <file1> <file2> ...` directly for approved items

---
type: note
status: inbox
created: 2026-04-01

# Sync chart step dots with swatch count

When the swatch count is reduced (e.g. 5), the curve charts still show dots for every lightness step. The chart steps should either only show dots at the lightness values that correspond to the displayed swatches, or visually distinguish swatch-aligned dots from the rest (e.g. larger/brighter dots for swatch steps, dimmed or hidden for others).

```

## Steps

### 1. Review Board State

Parse the pre-loaded board data above. Identify:
- All items with `status: backlog` or `status: current` (candidates for completion)
- Epics and their child tasks (via `parent` field)
- Blocked items and their blockers
- Items with terminal status (`done`, `processed`) that should be archived

### 2. Gather Codebase Context

- Run `git log --oneline` for recent commits (last 30) to understand what work has been done
- Only Read full file bodies for tasks whose status is `backlog` or `current` AND whose title suggests overlap with recent commits
- Use the context budget escalation (Glob → Grep → Read with limit → Full read) for any additional lookups

### 3. Detect Completed Work

For each task with `status: backlog` or `status: current`, check if the work described has already been done:

- Look at recent commits for evidence the work was completed
- Check the codebase for the feature/fix the task describes
- If the work is done, flag it for status update to `done`

### 4. Detect New Work Needed

Scan for things that should become tasks but aren't tracked yet:

- Check recent commits for TODOs or FIXMEs introduced in code
- Look at open epics — are there obvious next steps not yet captured as tasks?
- Check if any `blocked_by` dependencies have been resolved (unblocking tasks)

### 5. Update Epic Progress

For each active epic:

- Check if all child tasks are `done` — if so, flag the epic as `done`
- Check if any child tasks are `current` — if so, the epic should be `current`
- Update the task list in the epic body if tasks have been added/removed

### 6. Check for Stale Items

- Tasks marked `current` but with no related commits in the last 30 days
- Epics in `planning` with no child tasks
- Resources stuck in `inbox` or `processing`

### 7. Present Changes

Present ALL proposed changes as a single summary before making any edits:

```
## Board Update Proposal

### Status Changes
- TASK-0003: backlog -> done (cache-decoded-image: implemented in commit a1b2c3d)
- EPIC-0001: planning -> current (has active child tasks)

### New Tasks Suggested
- "Fix FIXME in preprocessing.rs line 42" (found in recent commit)

### Ready to Archive
- TASK-0003: done
- NOTE-0002: processed

### Stale Items
- TASK-0007: marked current but no activity in 45 days

### Unblocked
- TASK-0005: blocker TASK-0003 is now done
```

**Avoid redundant invocations** — if this skill was already invoked earlier in the session and the board has not been modified since, reuse the previous board data instead of invoking again.

Ask the user to approve, reject, or cherry-pick which changes to apply. Then execute only the approved changes:

- **Status updates**: Use `/board:board-edit` to update status fields
- **New tasks**: Invoke `/board:board-add` for each approved new task
- **Archiving**: Run `bash /Users/grant/Documents/Projects/BoardPM/plugin/scripts/archive-batch.sh ./board <file1> <file2> ...` directly for approved items

```bash

- **Avoid redundant invocations** — if this skill was already invoked earlier in the session and the board has not been modified since, reuse the previous board data instead of invoking again
- Files keep their original filename and ID after archiving
- Archived items remain searchable via grep and git log
- Links from other docs (e.g., `blocked_by`, `parent`) still work since the ID is stable
- ADRs are NOT archived — they stay in `board/planning/` regardless of status since they are immutable historical records that should remain easily discoverable
